meta.stack: Laravel 12 + Inertia.js + React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS v4 + Laravel Cashier (Stripe)

executive_summary: The Laravel React Starter template is a production-grade SaaS boilerplate with exceptional coverage across billing, auth, admin, webhooks, feature flags, and email marketing. Code quality is high, test coverage is comprehensive (90+ tests, 14 billing test files, 25 admin test files), and the architecture follows Laravel best practices. Four P1 issues require attention before production launch: (1) BillingService Redis lock silently degrades to non-atomic when CACHE_STORE is not Redis; (2) Default QUEUE_CONNECTION=database creates a single-database SPOF for billing operations; (3) Legal pages render placeholder content visibly in production without replacement; and (4) No database backup strategy is implemented. The remaining 27 P2 and 20 P3 findings are quality improvements that should be addressed systematically post-launch.

1.summary: Security posture is strong: CSRF exemption for Stripe webhook is correctly scoped, webhook signature verification uses hash_equals with replay-protection, billing uses Redis locks, rate limiting is comprehensive. Four issues found: the database cache driver (default) does not support atomic locks—if Redis is not configured the BillingService lock silently degrades to a non-atomic file/database lock, creating a race condition. The /health endpoint allows query-parameter token auth via health.allow_query_token config (deprecated path exposed). Blog content DOMPurify allows 'class' attribute in ALLOWED_ATTR which is fine for static content but the inline comment warns it should be removed for user content. The StripeWebhookController skips signature verification in local environment only, but preview environments that are publicly reachable may receive forged Stripe events.

findings.title: BillingService Redis lock degrades silently to non-atomic lock when CACHE_STORE is not Redis
findings.description: BillingService::withLock() calls Cache::lock(), but the default CACHE_STORE in .env.example is 'database'. The database cache driver in Laravel does NOT support atomic locks. If the operator forgets to configure CACHE_STORE=redis, all billing locks are non-atomic, eliminating concurrent subscription mutation protection.

implementation.approach: Add a boot-time assertion in AppServiceProvider that validates the cache driver supports atomic locks when billing is enabled.
changes: Add boot check: if billing enabled and cache driver is not redis, warn/throw
Update .env.example comment to make Redis mandatory for production billing
verification: php artisan config:show cache.default shows redis on production

findings.title: Health endpoint accepts token via query string when HEALTH_ALLOW_QUERY_TOKEN=true
findings.description: HealthCheckController supports a ?token= query parameter. Query string tokens appear in server access logs, proxy logs, and browser history.

implementation.approach: Remove the query-token code path entirely. Bearer header is sufficient.
changes: Remove allow_query_token config key and query-token branch in isAuthorized()
verification: Existing HealthCheckTest passes without query token tests

findings.title: Blog/Show DOMPurify ALLOWED_ATTR includes 'class' — comment warns it should be removed for user content
findings.description: Blog/Show.tsx sanitizes markdown-rendered HTML with DOMPurify. The ALLOWED_ATTR list includes 'class'. The inline comment explicitly notes to remove class if user-generated content is ever introduced.

implementation.approach: Remove 'class' from ALLOWED_ATTR. Static Markdown does not need CSS classes.
changes: Remove 'class' from ALLOWED_ATTR in Blog/Show.tsx
verification: Blog renders correctly without class attribute

findings.title: StripeWebhookController skips signature verification in local environment
findings.description: The StripeWebhookController constructor only enforces STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET in non-local environments. Preview environments that are publicly reachable may receive forged Stripe events.

implementation.approach: Change condition to check only local: if (!app()->isLocal() && empty(...))
changes: Change environment check from 'not local' to 'is production'
verification: Preview deployment rejects forged webhooks

2.summary: Architecture is well-structured with clear service boundaries, Form Requests, Redis-locked billing, and queued jobs for external calls. Three issues found: HandleInertiaRequests performs up to 4 extra queries per request with 60-second cache; DashboardController has inline login-streak logic; BroadcastAnnouncementJob has $tries=1 with no retry.

findings.title: HandleInertiaRequests executes up to 4 extra queries per request for notifications and limit warnings
findings.description: The share() method in HandleInertiaRequests fires lazy closures for notifications_unread_count and limit_warnings on every authenticated Inertia page load. Cache cold misses generate 3+ extra queries per user per minute.

implementation.approach: Increase TTL to 5 minutes or move notification count to a dedicated API endpoint polled by the frontend.
changes: Increase limit_warnings cache TTL from 60 to 300 seconds
verification: Query count tests still pass

findings.title: DashboardController contains inline business logic (login streak) that queries audit_logs directly
findings.description: getLoginStreak() in DashboardController performs complex date-window calculation against audit_logs with no caching. This is business logic that belongs in a service.

implementation.approach: Move getLoginStreak to CustomerHealthService::calculateLoginStreak() with caching.
changes: Move logic to CustomerHealthService
Add cache with 5-minute TTL
verification: DashboardControllerTest passes

findings.title: BroadcastAnnouncementJob has $tries=1 making broadcast failures permanent
findings.description: BroadcastAnnouncementJob iterates users in chunks of 200 and notifies each. $tries=1 means a transient mail failure will not be retried, silently leaving some users without the announcement.

implementation.approach: Increase $tries to 3 and track delivery in EmailSendLog so retried jobs skip already-notified users.
changes: Increase tries to 3
Add EmailSendLog idempotency guard
verification: Admin notification test covers partial-failure scenario

3.summary: PHPStan baseline contains 499 suppressed errors including two confirmed real type bugs in trial commands. Plans.php display prices can silently diverge from Stripe. ExportController route name is misleadingly broad. Sitemap has hardcoded lastmod dates.

findings.title: PHPStan baseline suppresses 499 errors including real type issues in billing and trial commands
findings.description: The phpstan-baseline.neon suppresses 499 type errors. Two confirmed real bugs: CheckExpiredTrials calls ->toISOString() on a string; SendTrialNudges passes string|null where Carbon|null is required.

evidence.proof: message: '#^Cannot call method toISOString\(\) on string\.$#' path: app/Console/Commands/CheckExpiredTrials.php

implementation.approach: Fix the real type errors first, then work through the baseline systematically.
changes: Fix CheckExpiredTrials: cast to Carbon before calling toISOString
Fix SendTrialNudges: use Carbon::parse() on trial_ends_at before passing
verification: phpstan-baseline.neon line count decreases
Commands still pass tests

findings.title: Plan display prices in plans.php can silently diverge from actual Stripe prices
findings.description: config/plans.php uses env() for display prices with a 'DISPLAY-ONLY' comment. When prices change in Stripe, the display price shown to users continues showing the old price until manually updated.

implementation.approach: Fetch the actual price from Stripe's API at deploy time and assert it matches the configured display price.
changes: Add a deploy-time validation artisan command that compares plans.php prices to Stripe
verification: Command exits non-zero if prices diverge

findings.title: Sitemap has hardcoded lastmod dates that will go stale
findings.description: SeoController::buildSitemap() uses hardcoded ISO 8601 lastmod strings. These must be manually updated after content changes.

implementation.approach: Store lastmod dates in a config file or drive them from git commit timestamps.
changes: Move lastmod into a config array or use now()->toISOString() for dynamic pages
verification: Sitemap XML contains current dates for recently modified pages

findings.title: ExportController route named 'export.users' is misleadingly named—it only exports the auth user's own record
findings.description: Route /export/users with name 'export.users' sounds like a bulk export but queries WHERE id = auth()->id(). Template consumers may add admin-level logic assuming broader scope, creating IDOR risk.

implementation.approach: Rename route to 'export.profile' or 'export.self' and rename controller method to self().
changes: Rename route and method to clarify single-user scope
verification: Route tests pass with new name

4.summary: Test suite is exceptionally comprehensive: 90+ tests covering billing, auth, admin, webhooks. Contract tests exist. Playwright E2E covers auth flows. Two gaps: AuthenticationTest uses PHPUnit syntax inconsistent with Pest suite; involuntary churn win-back notification dispatch path lacks explicit test coverage.

findings.title: Base AuthenticationTest uses PHPUnit-style syntax inconsistent with Pest suite
findings.description: tests/Feature/Auth/AuthenticationTest.php uses public function test_*() PHPUnit syntax while all other tests use Pest's it()/test() syntax.

implementation.approach: Convert to Pest syntax: replace class-based tests with top-level it()/test() calls.
changes: Convert AuthenticationTest.php to Pest syntax
verification: php artisan test --filter AuthenticationTest passes

findings.title: Missing test coverage for involuntary churn win-back notification dispatch path
findings.description: StripeWebhookController::handleCustomerSubscriptionDeleted() dispatches InvoluntaryChurnWinBackNotification when cancellation_reason === 'payment_failed'. This conditional notification dispatch is not explicitly asserted in tests.

implementation.approach: Add a test in StripeWebhookTest that sends a subscription.deleted payload with cancellation_details.reason=payment_failed and asserts the notification was dispatched.
changes: Add test for involuntary churn win-back notification dispatch
verification: New test passes and demonstrates notification is sent only once

5.summary: Accessibility implementation is above average: Radix UI primitives provide focus trapping, keyboard navigation, and ARIA roles. Two issues: Dashboard health score 'At Risk' label uses text-orange-500 without dark mode variant; flash toasts lack differentiated ARIA roles for success vs error severity.

findings.title: Dashboard health score 'At Risk' label uses text-orange-500 without dark mode variant
findings.description: Dashboard.tsx returns {label: 'At Risk', color: 'text-orange-500'} for health scores 26-50. text-orange-500 has insufficient contrast on dark backgrounds.

implementation.approach: Change to 'text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400' for WCAG 4.5:1 contrast on both themes.
changes: Update Dashboard.tsx healthLabel function to use dark mode paired colors
verification: Color contrast checker passes at 4.5:1 in both light and dark modes

findings.title: Flash toast notifications lack differentiated ARIA roles for success vs error severity
findings.description: Sonner renders all toasts with role='status' by default. Error toasts should use role='alert' (assertive) to immediately interrupt screen reader focus.

implementation.approach: Pass aria prop to Sonner or use toast.error() with { role: 'alert' } call.
changes: Update useFlashToasts.ts to pass {role: 'alert'} for error and warning toasts
verification: Screen reader announces error toasts immediately

6.summary: Copy quality is generally strong. Three issues: dunning email plan name resolution always falls back to 'your plan' due to wrong config key; retention coupon success message hardcodes '20%'; legal pages render placeholder content with in-production visible disclaimer.

findings.title: Dunning email plan name resolver uses wrong config key 'plans.tiers' instead of top-level tier keys
findings.description: SendDunningReminders::resolvePlanName() iterates config('plans.tiers', []) but plans.php has no 'tiers' key—plans are stored as top-level keys (pro, pro_team, team, enterprise). The resolver always returns 'your plan' in every dunning email.

evidence.proof: $plans = config('plans.tiers', []); // should be config('plans') minus hierarchy/trial keys

implementation.approach: Use app(BillingService::class)->resolveTierFromPrice() then config('plans.{tier}.name') to get the display name.
changes: Replace resolvePlanName() with BillingService::resolveTierFromPrice() + config lookup
verification: Test that dunning email contains correct plan name for a known price ID

findings.title: Retention coupon success message hardcodes '20%' discount percentage
findings.description: SubscriptionController::applyRetentionCoupon() returns a hardcoded '20%' success message. The actual coupon is configured via plans.retention_coupon_id and could be any percentage.

evidence.proof: return response()->json(['message' => 'Discount applied! Your 20% discount has been added to your subscription.']);

implementation.approach: Add plans.retention_coupon_percent config key and use config value in success message.
changes: Add plans.retention_coupon_percent config key
Use config value in success message
verification: Success message reflects configured percentage

findings.title: Legal pages render placeholder content with an in-UI warning—not production-ready
findings.description: LegalContent.tsx renders a destructive Alert reading 'Template Content — Do Not Use As-Is' unconditionally. A buyer who forgets to replace this will publish a page with a red warning banner visible to users and search engines.

implementation.approach: Gate the disclaimer behind APP_ENV=local check. Add a pre-launch checklist item to replace legal content.
changes: Show disclaimer only when APP_ENV=local
Add legal content to init.sh checklist
verification: Production build does not render the red disclaimer

7.summary: Design implementation is polished: dark mode via CSS custom properties, loading states use LoadingButton, empty states use EmptyState component, error boundaries implemented. Two issues: Contact page success state uses hardcoded color classes instead of design system Alert; admin notifications list has no subject truncation.

findings.title: Contact page success alert uses raw div with hardcoded color classes instead of design system Alert
findings.description: Contact.tsx renders success state as a custom div with hardcoded green color classes. The design system has an Alert component with variant='success'.

evidence.proof: className="mb-6 rounded-lg border border-green-200 bg-green-50 p-4 text-sm text-green-800 dark:border-green-800 dark:bg-green-950 dark:text-green-200"

implementation.approach: Replace custom div with <Alert variant='success'><AlertDescription>...</AlertDescription></Alert>.
changes: Replace Contact.tsx success div with Alert component
verification: Contact success state visually matches other success states

findings.title: Admin Notifications Dashboard notification body has no display truncation in list view
findings.description: The admin notifications list renders n.subject directly with no explicit truncation, which can cause layout overflow for long notification bodies.

implementation.approach: Wrap subject in TruncateWithTooltip component (already exists in ui/) or add truncate CSS class.
changes: Wrap n.subject in <TruncateWithTooltip maxWidth={300}>
verification: Long subject lines do not overflow table cells

8.summary: Design system is well-constructed: Tailwind v4 CSS-first with full semantic token set. Two minor issues: ThemeProvider has hardcoded hex fallbacks that bypass design tokens; NPS responses index uses raw Tailwind colors instead of semantic success/warning/destructive tokens.

findings.title: ThemeProvider has hardcoded hex fallbacks that bypass the design token system
findings.description: ThemeProvider.tsx falls back to '#0f1318' and '#1D4ED8'. If design tokens change, these fallbacks return incorrect values.

implementation.approach: Remove the hardcoded fallbacks. If the CSS property is empty, return an empty string.
changes: Remove hex fallbacks from ThemeProvider.tsx CSS variable reads
verification: Theme switching still works correctly

findings.title: NPS responses index uses paired raw Tailwind color classes instead of semantic success token
findings.description: Admin/NpsResponses/Index.tsx uses 'text-green-600 dark:text-green-400', 'text-yellow-600 dark:text-yellow-400', 'text-red-600 dark:text-red-400' for NPS score coloring instead of --success, --warning, --destructive tokens.

implementation.approach: Replace with 'text-success', 'text-warning', 'text-destructive' semantic tokens.
changes: Update NPS score color classes to semantic tokens
verification: NPS score colors adapt correctly in both light and dark themes

9.summary: GDPR infrastructure is solid with cookie consent audit trail, data export, signed URL unsubscribe, and IP anonymization. Four issues: legal pages show placeholder disclaimer unconditionally in production; marketing consent not fully persisted server-side; audit log retention (90 days) too short for auth security events; PaymentFailedNotification incorrectly includes marketing unsubscribe link for a required service communication.

findings.title: Terms and Privacy pages contain placeholder legal text with in-production visible disclaimer
findings.description: LegalContent.tsx renders a red destructive Alert reading 'Template Content — Do Not Use As-Is' unconditionally. Under GDPR, a valid and specific privacy policy is required before processing personal data.

implementation.approach: Gate the disclaimer behind APP_ENV=local check. Add legal content replacement to init.sh checklist.
changes: Add {process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' && <TemplateDisclaimer />} conditional
Add legal content replacement to init.sh checklist output
verification: Production build does not render disclaimer alert

findings.title: Cookie consent is not fully persisted server-side—marketing consent not stored in database
findings.description: ConsentController::store() only persists analytics_consent to UserSetting. The marketing_consent decision is only logged. For GDPR, all consent decisions should be independently auditable per user.

implementation.approach: Also persist marketing consent as a UserSetting key. Add a consent_id UUID and return it in the API response.
changes: Add setSetting for 'marketing_consent' key
Generate and return consent_id UUID
verification: PersonalDataExportController exports both consent settings

findings.title: Audit log retention default (90 days) may not meet GDPR minimum for authentication events
findings.description: Single 90-day retention period applies to all event types. Some jurisdictions require auth events retained up to 12 months for security incident investigation.

implementation.approach: Implement tiered retention: security events (auth.*) retained 365 days, all other events 90 days.
changes: Update PruneAuditLogs command to use tiered retention
Add AUDIT_LOG_SECURITY_RETENTION_DAYS config
verification: Security events not pruned before 365 days

findings.title: PaymentFailedNotification and billing notifications include marketing unsubscribe link on required service communications
findings.description: HasUnsubscribeLink is included in PaymentFailedNotification. Transactional billing emails should NOT have marketing unsubscribe links—they are required service communications. The 'marketing_emails' setting may then suppress mandatory billing notifications.

implementation.approach: Remove HasUnsubscribeLink from PaymentFailed, PaymentActionRequired, PaymentRecovered, RefundProcessed.
changes: Remove HasUnsubscribeLink from transactional billing notifications
Add billing_emails UserSetting key distinct from marketing_emails
verification: Billing notifications send regardless of marketing_emails setting

10.summary: Operational resilience is strong with Redis locks, DB transactions, out-of-order webhook rejection, and retry handlers. Three risks: default QUEUE_CONNECTION=database creates SPOF; StripeWebhookController uses app() service location inside handlers with silent catch-all; no circuit breaker on Stripe API calls.

findings.title: Default QUEUE_CONNECTION=database creates single-database SPOF and performance coupling
findings.description: The default .env.example sets QUEUE_CONNECTION=database. Queue workers, web requests, and cache share the same database. A slow queue increases DB connection pressure, slowing web response times.

evidence.proof: QUEUE_CONNECTION=database # Production: Use redis for all three

implementation.approach: Change .env.example default to QUEUE_CONNECTION=redis. Add boot-time warning when billing is enabled and queue driver is 'database'.
changes: Update .env.example default to QUEUE_CONNECTION=redis
Add production check in AppServiceProvider
verification: Production deployment guide documents Redis requirement

findings.title: StripeWebhookController uses app() service location inside handlers instead of constructor injection
findings.description: Handler methods call app(LifecycleService::class) via service locator. If these classes fail to resolve, the exception is swallowed by a bare catch(\Throwable) block, silently failing lifecycle transitions.

implementation.approach: Log errors in catch blocks: catch(\Throwable $e) { Log::error('lifecycle_transition_failed', [...]) }
changes: Log errors in catch blocks
Consider constructor injection if Cashier's WebhookController supports it
verification: Lifecycle transition failures appear in application log

findings.title: No circuit breaker on Stripe API calls—all failures retry indefinitely
findings.description: BillingService wraps all Stripe operations in locks and transactions but has no circuit breaker. During a Stripe API outage, all billing mutations exhaust Redis lock timeouts.

implementation.approach: Check Stripe status before mutations using a cached flag. Open circuit after 3 consecutive 5xx errors within 60 seconds.
changes: Add pre-flight Stripe availability check before mutations
verification: During Stripe outage, appropriate user-facing message shown without excessive retries

11.summary: CI pipeline is comprehensive with parallel tests, TypeScript checks, Pint, PHPStan, composer audit, npm audit, and Playwright E2E. Two issues: PHPStan and Pint only run on PRs, not direct pushes to main; Sentry is commented out by default with no boot-time warning.

findings.title: PHPStan and Pint code quality checks do not run on push to main—only on PRs
findings.description: The code-quality job has `if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'`. Direct pushes bypass PHPStan and Pint.

implementation.approach: Remove the `if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'` condition from the code-quality job.
changes: Remove PR-only condition from code-quality job in ci.yml
verification: Direct push to main triggers PHPStan and Pint checks

findings.title: Sentry error tracking is commented out by default—no boot-time warning when disabled in production
findings.description: .env.example has SENTRY_LARAVEL_DSN commented out. No boot-time check warns operators when Sentry DSN is unset in production.

implementation.approach: Add a production boot check in AppServiceProvider that logs a warning when APP_ENV=production and SENTRY_LARAVEL_DSN is not configured.
changes: Add Log::warning in AppServiceProvider when production + no Sentry DSN
Uncomment Sentry vars in .env.example
verification: Production server log contains warning when Sentry not configured

12.summary: Documentation quality is exceptional: comprehensive CLAUDE.md, 14 docs/ files, PHPDoc blocks on services. Two minor gaps: README screenshots section is empty; no generated OpenAPI spec despite Scribe being configured.

findings.title: README.md screenshots section is empty—no visual onboarding for new buyers
findings.description: README.md has a Screenshots section with four commented-out image references for Auth Flow, Admin Panel, Billing, and Onboarding.

evidence.proof: <!-- Add screenshots of key features here -->

implementation.approach: Add actual screenshots of key flows. Store in docs/screenshots/.
changes: Add screenshots to docs/screenshots/
Uncomment README.md image references
verification: README renders screenshots on GitHub

findings.title: No OpenAPI/Scribe API documentation generated despite api_docs feature flag existing
findings.description: config/features.php has an api_docs feature flag for Scribe, and routes/api.php has @group and @response PHPDoc annotations. However, there is no generated spec and the feature defaults to false.

implementation.approach: Run php artisan scribe:generate and commit the generated docs to docs/api/.
changes: Generate and commit initial API docs
Add scribe:generate to README setup steps
verification: Generated docs are accessible when FEATURE_API_DOCS=true

13.summary: SEO implementation is solid: dynamic sitemap, robots.txt, OG tags, structured data, BreadcrumbJsonLd. Three issues: Welcome and Pricing pages missing canonical links and og:url; Welcome page lacks SoftwareApplication structured data for rich results; Contact and About pages have no meta description.

findings.title: Welcome page and Pricing page missing canonical link tag and og:url meta property
findings.description: Welcome.tsx has OG tags but no canonical link or og:url. Without canonical links, search engines may index query-parameter variants as separate pages.

evidence.proof: grep canonical Welcome.tsx returns no results

implementation.approach: Pass canonicalUrl as an Inertia prop from controllers, then render canonical link and og:url in Head.
changes: Add canonicalUrl prop to WelcomeController and PricingController
Add canonical link and og:url in Welcome.tsx and Pricing.tsx Head
verification: curl -s /pricing | grep canonical returns correct URL

findings.title: Welcome page has no SoftwareApplication structured data for Google rich results
findings.description: The Welcome page has OG tags but no JSON-LD structured data. SoftwareApplication schema enables Google rich results (star ratings, pricing in search).

implementation.approach: Add SoftwareApplication JSON-LD schema to Welcome.tsx Head with name, description, applicationCategory, offers, and aggregateRating.
changes: Add SoftwareApplication JSON-LD to Welcome.tsx
verification: Google Rich Results Test passes for homepage

findings.title: Contact page and About page have no meta description
findings.description: Contact.tsx and About.tsx have no meta description tag. Meta descriptions impact click-through rates from SERPs.

evidence.proof: grep 'meta.*description' Contact.tsx returns no results in Head

implementation.approach: Add <meta name='description' content='...' /> to the Head component in Contact.tsx and About.tsx.
changes: Add meta description to Contact.tsx and About.tsx
verification: Pages pass basic SEO audit in Lighthouse

14.summary: GTM infrastructure is sophisticated: 196 analytics events across 14 categories, 9 marketing/comparison/feature pages, multi-step email sequences (7 types, 18+ variants), trial system, A/B pricing variants, and granular CTA tracking. Five gaps: GA4 Measurement Protocol is disabled by default with no activation path documented; no retargeting pixel infrastructure; blog platform has no seeded default content creating empty experience at launch; NPS survey has eligible endpoint but no lifecycle-integrated dispatch command; missing explicit free-to-paid conversion funnel events for analytics dashboards.

findings.title: GA4 Measurement Protocol disabled by default with no documented activation path
findings.description: GA4_MEASUREMENT_PROTOCOL_ENABLED defaults to false in config/services.php and is not documented in .env.example as a required production variable. Server-side event tracking (billing conversions, subscription events) requires the Measurement Protocol to be active. Without it, backend analytics events are silently dropped—buyers may not realize they have no funnel visibility.

evidence.note: GA4 Measurement Protocol disabled by default
GA4 vars present but Measurement Protocol flag not documented as required

implementation.approach: Add GA4_MEASUREMENT_PROTOCOL_ENABLED to the analytics section of .env.example with a comment explaining it is required for server-side event tracking. Add a note in the launch checklist (scripts/init.sh) to configure GA4 before launch.
changes: Add GA4_MEASUREMENT_PROTOCOL_ENABLED=true to .env.example analytics section with explanation comment
Add GA4 configuration step to scripts/init.sh launch checklist
verification: php artisan tinker: app(AnalyticsGateway::class)->send(...) successfully posts to GA4 MP endpoint

findings.title: No retargeting pixel infrastructure on marketing pages
findings.description: The marketing pages (Welcome, Pricing, Compare, Features, Guides) have Google Analytics via GA4 but no retargeting pixel support (Facebook/Meta Pixel, Google Ads remarketing tag). Buyers who want paid acquisition retargeting campaigns have no scaffolding to add pixel code in a GDPR-compliant way. The existing cookie consent system supports an 'advertising' category but no pixel code is wired up.

evidence.note: Only GA4 script injected; no advertising pixel integration
Cookie consent supports 'advertising' category but no pixel loads on consent

implementation.approach: Add conditional pixel loading in app.blade.php gated on: (1) the new FEATURE_RETARGETING env var, and (2) the 'marketing' cookie consent category. Wire Facebook Pixel and/or Google Ads remarketing tag loads after consent. Store pixel IDs in config/services.php.
changes: Add META_PIXEL_ID and GOOGLE_ADS_ID to config/services.php and .env.example
Add GDPR-gated pixel load block in app.blade.php following GA4 pattern
Document in init.sh checklist
verification: Pixel fires only after user accepts marketing cookies

findings.title: Blog platform has no seeded default content—buyers launch with an empty blog
findings.description: routes/web.php registers /blog and /blog/{slug} routes, resources/content/blog/ holds markdown files. The repository ships with no example blog posts. A buyer who deploys without reading docs will have a /blog page with empty content in production, which hurts SEO and creates a poor first impression for potential customers visiting the site.

evidence.note: No markdown blog posts committed to repository
evidence.proof: ls resources/content/blog/ returns empty or does not exist

evidence.note: Blog routes registered without content guard

implementation.approach: Ship at least 2-3 example blog posts in resources/content/blog/ with realistic SaaS developer content (e.g., 'Building your first SaaS with Laravel 12', 'Setting up Stripe billing'). Alternatively, add a blog.enabled feature flag that disables blog routes when no content exists.
changes: Add 2-3 example blog posts to resources/content/blog/
Or add FEATURE_BLOG env var and gate blog routes
verification: /blog returns content, not empty page

findings.title: NPS survey has eligible endpoint but no lifecycle-integrated scheduled dispatch
findings.description: routes/web.php registers GET /nps/eligible and the NPS feature exists (routes, models, admin panel at /admin/nps-responses). However, no scheduled command was found that automatically dispatches NPS surveys based on lifecycle stage (e.g., 30 days after ACTIVATED, or 14 days after subscription start). Without automated dispatch, NPS data collection requires manual triggering.

evidence.note: NPS eligible endpoint exists but no corresponding dispatch command
No SendNpsSurvey or similar command found in commands directory
evidence.proof: ls app/Console/Commands/ shows no NPS dispatch command

implementation.approach: Create a SendNpsSurvey artisan command that targets users who reached ACTIVATED or PAYING lifecycle stage N days ago and have not received an NPS survey in the last 90 days. Schedule it daily in bootstrap/app.php routes/console.php.
changes: Create app/Console/Commands/SendNpsSurveys.php
Schedule daily in routes/console.php or bootstrap/app.php
verification: NPS surveys dispatched to qualifying users on schedule

findings.title: Missing explicit free-to-paid conversion funnel events for analytics dashboard
findings.description: 196 analytics events cover auth, billing, and engagement individually, but there is no dedicated funnel event sequence that connects: landing_page_visit → signup → onboarding_completed → trial_started → subscription_created. Without this chain, building a conversion funnel dashboard in GA4 or a data warehouse requires joining 5 separate event types with complex logic.

evidence.note: Events exist but no explicit funnel_step or funnel_position property
evidence.proof: AUTH_REGISTER, ONBOARDING_COMPLETED, TRIAL_STARTED, BILLING_SUBSCRIPTION_CREATED exist as separate events

implementation.approach: Add a funnel_step property to key conversion events (register, onboarding_completed, trial_started, checkout_completed) so GA4 exploration reports can show a single funnel. Alternatively, document the recommended GA4 funnel configuration in docs/.
changes: Add funnel_step parameter to AUTH_REGISTER, ONBOARDING_COMPLETED, TRIAL_STARTED, BILLING_CHECKOUT_COMPLETED events
Document GA4 funnel configuration in docs/ANALYTICS_SETUP.md
verification: GA4 funnel exploration shows 4-step conversion funnel

15.summary: Growth funnel is well-architected: 3-step onboarding wizard, engagement scoring, re-engagement sequences, dunning with 3-email escalation, PQL threshold tracking (50/80/100%), win-back flow, lifecycle stage tracking with transition history, and UpgradePrompt component. Five issues: CheckExpiredTrials has a confirmed real type error (toISOString on string, already in PHPStan baseline); TrackLastActivity middleware writes to a field that does not exist in the migration; expansion nudge has no data-driven trigger; win-back command doesn't guard against billing disabled; EngagementScoringService feature adoption scoring checks static conditions that may not reflect actual feature usage.

findings.title: CheckExpiredTrials command calls ->toISOString() on string — confirmed runtime crash
findings.description: app/Console/Commands/CheckExpiredTrials.php calls ->toISOString() on a value that PHPStan confirms is a string (it is retrieved from the database as a string, not cast to Carbon). This will throw a fatal error when the command processes any expired trial, breaking the trial expiration flow entirely.

evidence.note: Confirmed by PHPStan baseline entry
evidence.proof: message: '#^Cannot call method toISOString\(\) on string\.$#' path: app/Console/Commands/CheckExpiredTrials.php

implementation.approach: Cast the trial_ends_at column to Carbon before calling any Carbon methods. Add a cast to the User model or use Carbon::parse().
changes: Wrap value with Carbon::parse($value) before calling ->toISOString()
Or add trial_ends_at to User $casts array as 'datetime'
verification: CheckExpiredTrials command runs without error on user with expired trial_ends_at

findings.title: TrackLastActivity middleware references last_active_at but field presence in users migration is unclear
findings.description: SendReEngagementEmails command uses `last_active_at` with a fallback to `last_login_at` then `created_at`. TrackLastActivity middleware is responsible for writing this field. If the users migration does not include a last_active_at column (and it is not in a separate migration), this middleware would produce silent errors or update no rows.

evidence.note: Middleware writes to last_active_at
Command reads last_active_at with fallback chain

implementation.approach: Verify the users migration includes last_active_at. If not, add a migration to add the nullable timestamp column with an index (for the WHERE last_active_at < ? query performance).
changes: Verify or add last_active_at column to users table
Add index on last_active_at for re-engagement query performance
verification: TrackLastActivity middleware successfully updates last_active_at
Re-engagement command targets users by last activity date correctly

findings.title: SendWinBackEmails command does not guard against billing feature being disabled
findings.description: SendWinBackEmails targets users with subscriptions in canceled/incomplete_expired states. If billing is disabled (FEATURE_BILLING=false), the subscriptions table exists but is empty, and the command will run without errors but query unnecessary tables. More critically, if a developer disables billing and the command is scheduled, it may produce confusing win-back emails for users who never had a subscription.

evidence.note: No billing feature flag check at command start

implementation.approach: Add a feature flag guard at the start of the handle() method: if (!config('features.billing.enabled')) { $this->info('Billing disabled, skipping.'); return 0; }
changes: Add feature flag guard to handle() method
verification: Command exits early when FEATURE_BILLING=false

findings.title: ExpansionNudgeNotification has no data-driven trigger—manually dispatched only
findings.description: ExpansionNudgeNotification exists and is sent via a generic 'your team is growing' email, but no command or listener dispatches it automatically based on actual team growth signals (e.g., user approaching team seat limit, onboarding multiple users). Without an automated trigger, the expansion nudge is dead code unless manually dispatched.

evidence.note: No SendExpansionNudge or similar command in commands directory
evidence.proof: grep -r 'ExpansionNudgeNotification' app/ returns only the notification class itself

implementation.approach: Add a PlanLimitService listener on the LIMIT_THRESHOLD_80 event that checks if the resource is 'seats' and dispatches ExpansionNudgeNotification. This connects the existing PQL threshold tracking to expansion revenue.
changes: Add expansion nudge dispatch to PQL threshold listener when resource is 'seats'
Or create SendExpansionNudges command that targets team-tier users near seat limit
verification: Expansion nudge sent when team is at 80% seat capacity

findings.title: EngagementScoringService feature adoption scoring may not reflect actual feature usage
findings.description: EngagementScoringService scores feature adoption by checking if user has tokens or webhook endpoints (existence checks, not usage frequency). A user who created a token once and never used it scores the same as an active API consumer. This can cause high-intent users to be mis-scored and receive wrong re-engagement variants.

evidence.note: Feature adoption scored by existence not usage frequency

implementation.approach: Add usage frequency dimension: count tokens used in last 30 days (via audit_logs with event=api_token.used) and webhook deliveries in last 30 days. Weight active usage higher than mere existence.
changes: Add audit_log-based API activity check to scoring
Add WebhookDelivery recency check to scoring
verification: Active API users score higher than inactive token holders

16.summary: Deployment infrastructure is well-built: vps-setup.sh is comprehensive (546 lines, covers Redis, MySQL, Supervisor, Scheduler, file permissions, optimization), vps-verify.sh provides actionable post-deploy verification, CI pipeline covers 6 job types. Six gaps: no database backup strategy; migrations run without artisan down/up creating downtime risk; SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE not set in defaults; E2E tests hardcode localhost:8000; Sentry performance monitoring disabled by default; vps-setup.sh has no atomic deploy support.

findings.title: No database backup strategy—no backup package, command, or scheduled job
findings.description: The codebase has no database backup mechanism. No spatie/laravel-backup package, no mysqldump command, no S3 export, and no scheduled backup job. A server failure or accidental migration would result in permanent data loss. The audit log pruning command deletes records permanently (no soft delete).

evidence.note: No backup package in dependencies
No backup command
evidence.proof: ls app/Console/Commands/ shows no BackupDatabase or similar

implementation.approach: Install spatie/laravel-backup. Configure daily database-only backups to S3 (using BACKUP_DISK=s3 env var). Keep 7 daily + 4 weekly backups. Schedule backup:run and backup:clean in routes/console.php.
changes: composer require spatie/laravel-backup
Add config/backup.php configuration
Add backup disk to config/filesystems.php
Schedule daily backup:run and backup:clean
Add BACKUP_* vars to .env.example
verification: php artisan backup:run --only-db completes successfully

findings.title: No zero-downtime deployment pattern—migrations run directly on live database during deploy
findings.description: vps-setup.sh runs `php artisan migrate --force` without putting the application in maintenance mode first. For migration-heavy deploys, users see errors during the window between code deploy and migration completion. The script has no artisan down/up wrapper or atomic file-swap.

evidence.note: Migrations run without maintenance mode

implementation.approach: Wrap the migration step in artisan down/up with a custom maintenance page. For additive migrations (add column, add table), this is often not needed—document which migrations require downtime. Alternatively, implement a deploy script with atomic symlink swapping (Deployer/Envoy).
changes: Add php artisan down --render=errors::503 before migrations
Add php artisan up after migrations complete
Add DEPLOY_MAINTENANCE_SECRET to .env.example
verification: Custom 503 page rendered during migration window

findings.title: SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE not set to true in .env.example defaults
findings.description: .env.example has SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE commented out. For staging/preview deployments over HTTPS, cookies will be sent without the Secure flag unless manually set. Combined with QUEUE_CONNECTION=database and other defaults, a developer cloning and deploying the repo without careful .env review ships with insecure session cookies.

evidence.note: SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE missing from production defaults section

implementation.approach: Add SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE=true to the production environment section of .env.example. Add a boot-time check in AppServiceProvider: if production and SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE is not true, log a security warning.
changes: Add SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE=true to .env.example production section
Add AppServiceProvider boot warning for production without secure cookies
verification: Set-Cookie header contains Secure flag in production

findings.title: E2E tests hardcode localhost:8000 — fail in non-standard CI or port configurations
findings.description: The E2E test suite (tests/e2e/) hardcodes http://localhost:8000 as the application URL. If CI runs on a different port, or a developer runs locally with php artisan serve --port=8080, all E2E tests fail with connection refused.

evidence.note: APP_URL hardcoded in E2E job

implementation.approach: Use the APP_URL environment variable as the base URL in playwright.config.ts. Default to http://localhost:8000 only if APP_URL is not set. This allows override in any CI environment.
changes: Update playwright.config.ts: baseURL: process.env.APP_URL || 'http://localhost:8000'
Document APP_URL override in testing docs
verification: E2E tests pass when APP_URL=http://localhost:9000

findings.title: Sentry performance monitoring disabled by default (traces_sample_rate=0.0) with no activation guidance
findings.description: config/sentry.php sets traces_sample_rate to 0.0 by default. Without performance monitoring enabled, Sentry captures errors only—no slow query detection, no N+1 identification, no transaction tracing. For a production SaaS, at least 0.1 (10%) sampling is recommended. The config comment says '0.0 to disable' but doesn't guide operators to enable it.

evidence.note: Performance monitoring disabled by default

implementation.approach: Change the default in .env.example to SENTRY_TRACES_SAMPLE_RATE=0.1 for production. Add a comment explaining the trade-off (higher = more data, higher Sentry quota usage). Add this to the launch checklist.
changes: Add SENTRY_TRACES_SAMPLE_RATE=0.1 to .env.example production section
Add to scripts/init.sh launch checklist
verification: Sentry dashboard shows performance data after production deploy

findings.title: vps-setup.sh has no atomic deploy support—file copies are not transactional
findings.description: vps-setup.sh runs optimization commands (config:cache, route:cache) and restarts Horizon inline during a live deploy. Between config:cache completing and route:cache completing, the application may serve requests with stale config but fresh routes. For atomic deploys, new code should be prepared in a staging directory and symlinked atomically.

evidence.note: Sequential optimization without atomic swap
evidence.proof: php artisan config:cache && php artisan route:cache && php artisan event:cache

implementation.approach: Cache all artifacts (config, routes, views, events) before putting the new code live. Use a releases/ directory with a current symlink, swapping the symlink atomically after all caches are warm.
changes: Add a prepare_release() function that caches config/routes/views before swap
Add atomic symlink swap: ln -sfn releases/new current
Document deploy flow in DEPLOYMENT.md
verification: Zero config/route mismatch errors during deploy window

17.summary: Admin panel is comprehensive: 25 admin controllers covering users (full CRUD, bulk actions, impersonation), billing stats (MRR, cohort retention, tier distribution), feature flag management (global and per-user overrides), audit log export, failed jobs, cache management, sessions, system info, roadmap, NPS responses, webhooks, tokens, and notifications. Four issues: audit log CSV export provides full metadata including PII without field filtering for non-super-admin; AdminUsersController::show() loads all audit logs and stage history without pagination limit; admin billing stats cache lacks invalidation on subscription webhooks; feature flag UI doesn't communicate that route-dependent flags cannot be enabled when env=false.

findings.title: Audit log CSV export provides full metadata JSON including PII without role-based field filtering
findings.description: AdminAuditLogController::export() explicitly documents that it exports full metadata for compliance audits. However, the export is available to all admin users (not just super_admin). For users with is_admin=true but not super_admin, this means they can download full audit logs including IP addresses, user agents, and metadata from all users—a privacy risk if the admin role is granted broadly.

evidence.note: Export available to all admins, no super_admin gate
evidence.proof: Route::get('/admin/audit-logs/export', ...) in routes/admin.php with middleware ['auth', 'verified', 'admin', 'throttle:60,1'] but no super_admin middleware

implementation.approach: Gate the audit log export route with super_admin middleware. For regular admins, provide a filtered export that redacts IP addresses and sensitive metadata fields.
changes: Add ->middleware('super_admin') to the audit-logs export route in routes/admin.php
Or create a sanitized export path for non-super-admin that omits IP and raw metadata
verification: Regular admin (is_admin=true, not super_admin) cannot access audit export

findings.title: AdminUsersController::show() loads all audit logs and stage history without row limit
findings.description: The user detail view in admin loads audit logs (recent activity) and stage_history for the specified user without a hard row limit. For active users with thousands of audit log entries, this can load unbounded data into memory and exceed the per-request query budget.

evidence.note: Audit logs loaded with no row cap

implementation.approach: Add ->limit(50) to the audit log query in AdminUsersController::show(). For stage history, add ->limit(20). Add a 'View all activity' link to the full audit log list filtered by user.
changes: Add ->limit(50) to audit log query in show()
Add ->limit(20) to stage_history query
Add link to full audit log filtered by user_id
verification: User detail page query count <= 8 even for users with 10,000+ audit entries

findings.title: Admin billing stats cache not invalidated on subscription webhook events
findings.description: AdminBillingStatsService caches dashboard stats (MRR, tier distribution, trial stats) with 5-minute TTL. StripeWebhookController handles subscription creation, cancellation, and resumption events but does not call Cache::forget() on the relevant AdminCacheKey entries. The CLAUDE.md review checklist explicitly requires this invalidation.

evidence.note: Webhook handlers do not invalidate billing stats cache
Explicit review checklist item requiring billing cache invalidation
evidence.proof: Billing mutations (subscribe, cancel, resume, swap) must invalidate BILLING_STATS and BILLING_TIER_DIST

implementation.approach: Add Cache::forget(AdminCacheKey::BILLING_STATS->value) and Cache::forget(AdminCacheKey::BILLING_TIER_DIST->value) to all subscription webhook handlers (created, deleted, updated, resumed).
changes: Add cache invalidation to handleCustomerSubscriptionCreated()
Add cache invalidation to handleCustomerSubscriptionDeleted()
Add cache invalidation to handleCustomerSubscriptionUpdated() if it exists
verification: Admin billing dashboard shows updated MRR within seconds of a new subscription

findings.title: Feature flag admin UI does not communicate that route-dependent flags cannot be enabled when env=false
findings.description: FeatureFlagService has a protected_flags list and a route_dependent_flags list—flags like billing, social_auth, and api_docs that require routes registered at boot time cannot be enabled at runtime if the env var is false (because the routes were never registered). The admin UI allows attempting to enable these flags and the DB override is created, but the feature remains inactive. This silent failure confuses admins.

evidence.note: Route-dependent flags cannot be enabled at runtime but this is not surfaced in UI

implementation.approach: In the feature flag admin UI, display a warning badge on route-dependent flags when the env var is false: 'Requires server restart (routes are registered at boot time)'. Pass an is_route_dependent and env_value prop from the controller.
changes: Add is_route_dependent and env_value to AdminFeatureFlagController response
Show warning badge in FeatureFlags admin UI for route-dependent flags when env=false
verification: Admin UI shows clear warning when attempting to enable billing via UI when FEATURE_BILLING=false in .env
